MS Brain 3D T1 Pre-contrast.description: 3D T1-weighted anatomic imaging for MS brain protocol (pre-contrast)
MS Brain 3D T1 Pre-contrast.detailed_description:
## 3D T1-Weighted Pre-Contrast (Anatomic)

This is the anatomic 3D T1-weighted sequence acquired BEFORE gadolinium contrast administration.

### Technical Parameters

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Sequence type | 3D IR-prepared T1 gradient echo (e.g., MPRAGE, BRAVO, 3D TFE) |
| Acquisition thickness | 1.0-1.5 mm isotropic |
| Reconstruction | 3 mm for clinical review |
| In-plane resolution | ≤1 x 1 mm |
| Coverage | Whole brain |
| Orientation | Subcallosal plane |

### Clinical Purpose

- Volumetric analysis (brain atrophy)
- Confirmation of juxtacortical and infratentorial lesions
- Anatomical reference
- Must be acquired BEFORE contrast for accurate volumetrics

### Important Notes

- **Acquire before gadolinium administration**
- T1 "black holes" are best assessed on 2D spin-echo sequences, not 3D IR-prepared images
- 3D acquisition enables multiplanar reformatting

### Reference

CMSC Task Force. AJNR Am J Neuroradiol 2016;37:394-401.

rules.name: Whole Brain Coverage
rules.description: MS brain imaging requires whole-brain coverage (typically >140mm in z-direction).
rules.implementation:
slice_thickness = value["SliceThickness"].iloc[0]
num_slices = value["Count"].iloc[0]
coverage = slice_thickness * num_slices

if coverage < 140:
    raise ValidationWarning(f"Brain coverage ({coverage:.0f} mm) may be insufficient for whole-brain MS imaging. Typical coverage is >140mm.")

MS Brain 3D FLAIR.description: 3D T2-FLAIR for MS lesion detection - primary sequence for white matter lesions
MS Brain 3D FLAIR.detailed_description:
## 3D T2-FLAIR (Primary Lesion Detection)

FLAIR is the primary sequence for detecting MS white matter lesions. The 3D acquisition enables multiplanar reformatting.

### Technical Parameters

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Sequence type | 3D T2-FLAIR |
| Acquisition thickness | 1.0-1.5 mm isotropic |
| Reconstruction | 3 mm for clinical review |
| In-plane resolution | ≤1 x 1 mm |
| Acquisition plane | Sagittal (for reformatting) |
| Coverage | Whole brain |

### Clinical Purpose

- **Primary sequence for MS lesion detection**
- Corpus callosum lesions (best seen on sagittal)
- Juxtacortical lesions (white matter touching cortex)
- Periventricular lesions (touching ventricles)
- Oval perivenular lesion configuration

### Key Lesion Locations for McDonald Criteria

| Location | Requirement |
|----------|-------------|
| Periventricular | ≥1 lesion touching ventricles |
| Juxtacortical | ≥1 lesion touching cortex |
| Infratentorial | ≥1 lesion |
| Spinal cord | ≥1 lesion |

**2 of 4 locations required for dissemination in space**

### Timing Note

FLAIR can be acquired during the 5-minute post-contrast delay (after gadolinium injection, before post-contrast T1).

### 2D Alternative

If 3D not available: 2D axial AND sagittal FLAIR at ≤3mm thickness, no gap.

### Reference

CMSC Task Force. AJNR Am J Neuroradiol 2016;37:394-401.

rules.name: Whole Brain Coverage
rules.description: MS brain imaging requires whole-brain coverage (typically >140mm in z-direction).
rules.implementation:
slice_thickness = value["SliceThickness"].iloc[0]
num_slices = value["Count"].iloc[0]
coverage = slice_thickness * num_slices

if coverage < 140:
    raise ValidationWarning(f"Brain coverage ({coverage:.0f} mm) may be insufficient for whole-brain MS imaging. Typical coverage is >140mm.")

MS Brain 2D FLAIR (Alternative).description: 2D T2-FLAIR alternative when 3D not available - requires both axial and sagittal acquisitions
MS Brain 2D FLAIR (Alternative).detailed_description:
## 2D T2-FLAIR (Alternative)

When 3D FLAIR is not available, 2D FLAIR should be acquired in **both axial AND sagittal** planes.

### Technical Parameters

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Sequence type | 2D T2-FLAIR |
| Slice thickness | ≤3 mm |
| Gap | **No gap** |
| Planes | Axial AND Sagittal |
| Coverage | Whole brain |

### Why Both Planes?

- **Sagittal**: Best for corpus callosum lesions (Dawson's fingers)
- **Axial**: Standard orientation for lesion counting and comparison

### No Gap Requirement

Slice gap can cause lesions to be missed. SpacingBetweenSlices should equal SliceThickness.

### Reference

CMSC Task Force. AJNR Am J Neuroradiol 2016;37:394-401.

rules.name: No Gap Between Slices
rules.description: 2D MS brain sequences should have no gap between slices. SpacingBetweenSlices should equal SliceThickness.
rules.implementation:
slice_thickness = value["SliceThickness"].iloc[0]
spacing = value["SpacingBetweenSlices"].iloc[0] if "SpacingBetweenSlices" in value.columns and not pd.isna(value["SpacingBetweenSlices"].iloc[0]) else slice_thickness

gap = spacing - slice_thickness
if gap > 0.5:
    raise ValidationWarning(f"Gap detected between slices ({gap:.1f} mm). MS protocols require no gap (SpacingBetweenSlices should equal SliceThickness).")
